Once upon a winter afternoon, little Dove loved flying. Every evening she'd glide across Oak trees. One rainy day she couldn'o soar.

She found an old owl.

"I owl you."

"Oh?"

"They call me Orion."

"Oh Orion.

That's my star."

"Why do you star shine?"

"Oh, you?"

"For me."

"You pretty?"

"Oh yes."

"Dove."

"Well, I think you're pretty."

"That's good."

"You know."

"How?"

"Oh. Owls see."

"That's funny."

"You know."

"Why?"

" because eyes."

"Oh."

Dove thinkred.

She'd love that star. Sometimes stars.

One evening she dreamed about moons.
